L1.area: User authentication and account management functionality

L2.description: A cryptographic weakness exists where password reset tokens are generated using a non-cryptographically secure pseudo-random number generator, making it feasible for remote attackers to predict token values and reset arbitrary user passwords.

L3.area: Password reset and account activation token generation within the user-facing login and session management handler
L3.description: A cryptographic weakness exists where password reset activation codes are constructed by feeding the output of a non-cryptographically secure pseudo-random number generator into an MD5 hash, making it feasible for remote attackers to predict the seed state, recover the token value, and reset arbitrary user passwords.

ground_truth.reason: Froxlor uses PHP's rand() function to generate password reset activation codes, which is not cryptographically secure and allows remote attackers to predict token values.
